group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.client.index.service.client.cache.idle.timeout
description: Idle timeout for cached index service clients. Entries that have not been accessed for this duration are evicted and their underlying gRPC channel is closed. Bounds long-tail resource accumulation when worker pods churn (every dead pod that the cache talked to would otherwise keep its channel alive forever). Default 1day favors avoiding spurious reconnects on sparse FUSE workloads at the cost of some log noise from dead-channel reconnect attempts (~720/day per evicted pod). Lower (e.g. 1h) for high-churn environments where many pods are recycled per day.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.batch.size
description: Batch size for listing directories. Default to 1000 which aligns with most object storage systems.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout
description: Per-batch inactivity timeout for the server-streaming listChildren RPC. When > 0, cancels the stream if the server fails to deliver the next batch within this window. Because batches arriving from the server reset the timer, this lets legitimate long listings run indefinitely while still failing fast on a worker that is stuck or blackholed mid-stream — the freeze the user sees is bounded by this value, not by the absolute listChildren deadline. Default 0 disables the inactivity timer entirely; the absolute listChildren deadline (alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout) is then the only bound. The default is conservative pending the worker-side cancellation handling tracked under AC-7212 — set to a positive value (e.g. 30sec) to opt in once the worker side is ready.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.client.index.service.list.timeout
description: Absolute deadline applied to the server-streaming listChildren RPC. Set generously because legitimate large-directory listings (e.g. prewarm of a million-file bucket) take real time; a stuck worker freezes the caller for up to this duration, but pre-fix behavior was an unbounded hang (AC-7179). Unary index service RPCs (addChild, removeChild, invalidate) use the generic alluxio.user.rpc.retry.max.duration timeout instead so they fail fast. Acts as a backstop alongside the per-batch inactivity timeout (alluxio.client.index.service.list.inactivity.timeout) — whichever fires first cancels the stream.

name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type
description: The behavior of loading metadata from UFS. When information about a path is requested and the path does not exist in Alluxio, metadata can be loaded from the UFS. Valid options are `ALWAYS`, `NEVER`, and `ONCE`. `ALWAYS` will always access UFS to see if the path exists in the UFS. `NEVER` will never consult the UFS. `ONCE` will access the UFS the "first" time (according to a cache), but not after that. This parameter is ignored if a metadata sync is performed, via the parameter "alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval"

name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.refresh.ahead.margin.ms
description: The time margin between when to refresh a file's metadata and when the snapshot of the file's metadata expires. The ahead-of-time refresh will reduce the chance when the snapshot has not yet expired, but is very close to expiry, and will probably expire considering the network delay and queuing times, etc. when a request is processed by the worker.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.user.file.metadata.sync.interval
description: The interval for syncing UFS metadata before invoking an operation on a path. -1 means no sync will occur. 0 means Alluxio will always sync the metadata of the path before an operation. If you specify a time interval, Alluxio will (best effort) not re-sync a path within that time interval. Syncing the metadata for a path must interact with the UFS, so it is an expensive operation. If a sync is performed for an operation, the configuration of "alluxio.user.file.metadata.load.type" will be ignored.

description: The minimum logical file size required to trigger the space amplification check for write cache compaction. If a file's logical size is smaller than this threshold, compaction will not be triggered regardless of its space amplification ratio. This prevents unnecessary I/O overhead from compacting small files where the reclaimed space would be negligible.

This value represents the allowed physical storage overhead relative to the logical file size as a percentage. For example, the default value of 50 means compaction is triggered when the physical file size reaches 150% of its logical size (i.e., the extra space occupied is 50% of the logical size). A value of 100 would mean compaction triggers when physical size is 200% of the logical size.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.inclusion.percent
description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Any worker whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is above this percentage is always kept in the candidate pool, in addition to the top fraction. This lets a worker keep receiving writes until it is (100 minus this value) percent full, so a lightly loaded cluster spreads writes across every worker instead of parking the lowest-ranked ones.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.min.remaining.percent
description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. Workers whose remaining pinned-cache capacity is below this percentage are dropped from the candidate pool (lowest-ratio first), but the pool is never reduced below the requested replica count.

group_title: 客户端文件系统与读写路径
name: alluxio.user.write.cache.pinned.capacity.policy.top.fraction
description: Used by the write-cache worker selection policy. The fraction [0, 1] of workers, ranked by remaining pinned-cache capacity, that always stay in the candidate pool (set to 0 to disable the floor and rely solely on the inclusion threshold). The emptiest workers up to this fraction are kept even when most of the cluster is filling up, so writes concentrate on the least-full nodes under pressure.

group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled
description: If true, the local page store writes staged pages with direct I/O (O_DIRECT), bypassing the OS page cache. Because the page store is itself a cache, buffered writes double-cache the data and pollute the page cache; direct I/O avoids that and reduces the CPU spent copying data into the kernel page cache, at the cost of an extra user-space copy into an aligned buffer. Only the staging write path is affected; metadata and append writes stay buffered. Direct I/O is only supported on Linux filesystems that allow O_DIRECT (e.g. ext4, xfs); on an unsupported filesystem the staged page write fails and the error is surfaced, so disable this option there. Applies to both the worker page store and the client-side cache.

group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.min.size
description: Minimum staged page write size for using direct I/O. When alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.direct.io.enabled is true, writes smaller than this threshold use the buffered write path instead. Set to 0 to allow direct I/O for all staged page writes.

group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.file.buckets

group_title: 客户端本地缓存 / 元数据缓存
name: alluxio.user.client.cache.local.store.put.staging.enabled
description: If true, LocalPageStore.put() writes the page to a staging file under the cache dir's .staging/ subdirectory and atomically renames it into place. Preserves snapshot consistency for in-flight POSIX-pinned zero-copy reads when the same page is concurrently rewritten (e.g. passive cache / re-load), and prevents leaving a 0-byte stub at the page path if the disk hits ENOSPC mid-write. If false, falls back to in-place O_TRUNC + write. Default true; disable only to mitigate a regression specific to staging-based writes.

group_title: Worker Page Store 存储引擎
name: alluxio.worker.data.load.streaming.enabled
description: If true (default), distributed load reads each file through a single per-partition stream (one ranged GET per partition on object stores) and inserts it one page at a time, so the UFS read granularity is decoupled from resident memory: direct memory is one page per concurrent load task, independent of the GET size. This is the same path for all file sizes. If false, falls back to the legacy buffered path, which reads up to per UFS read and holds it in direct memory while inserting its pages. Both produce identical on-disk results; set to false as a per-worker rollback knob.

name: alluxio.worker.http.server.connection.idle.timeout
description: Idle timeout for connections on the worker HTTP server (the :28080 REST / OCI-mirror pipeline). When a connection has neither read nor written for this long, it is closed, reclaiming file descriptors from clients that vanished without sending a TCP RST (container OOM, NAT drop). Active transfers keep the channel non-idle so they are not affected. 0 disables idle detection.

name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.backpressure.timeout
description: Maximum time a heavy-pool thread will wait for the Netty channel to become writable (backpressure) before aborting with an IOException. Prevents heavy-pool threads from being pinned indefinitely by hung or slow TCP clients. Set to 0 to disable the timeout (not recommended).

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.core.thread.number
description: Core thread number for async heavy thread pool. Defaults to the same value as the maximum thread number so that ThreadPoolExecutor never calls Thread.start() from the caller (event loop) thread on the hot path. Sized to absorb concurrent HEAVY-tag S3 ops (GetObject, PutObject, CopyObject, UploadPartCopy) under high connection counts (~512 MB stack).

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.maximum.thread.number
description: Maximum thread number for async heavy thread pool. Keep this >= peak concurrent S3 connections handling HEAVY ops; under-sizing surfaces as s3_api_async_rejections{kind="heavy"} and 503 SlowDown.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.heavy.pool.queue.size
description: Queue size for async heavy thread pool. Bounded so that bursts above sustainable load surface as RejectedExecutionException back-pressure rather than unbounded queue growth.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.core.thread.number
description: Core thread number for async light thread pool. Defaults to the same value as the maximum thread number so that ThreadPoolExecutor never calls Thread.start() from the caller (event loop) thread on the hot path.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.maximum.thread.number
description: Maximum thread number for async light thread pool.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.light.pool.queue.size
description: Queue size for async light thread pool. Bounded so that bursts above sustainable load surface as RejectedExecutionException back-pressure rather than unbounded queue growth.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.max.part.number
description: Maximum number of prefetched parts the worker-side S3 prefetch pool keeps ahead per active read stream. Combined with this caps the lookahead window per stream.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.part.length
description: Bytes per prefetched part on the worker-side S3 prefetch pool. Default 4 MiB matches the pre-PR worker behaviour (via the client FAST pool); smaller parts reduce first-byte latency on cold reads at the cost of more S3 GETs per prefetched window.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.queue.size
description: Capacity of the bounded fallback queue used by the worker-side S3 prefetch pool once all threads are busy. Submissions beyond this bound are rejected; the foreground UFS read still serves the request.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.prefetch.threads.max
description: Maximum number of threads in the worker-side S3 prefetch pool. Used by S3RangeReader for response prefetch and by S3NettyObjectTask for asynchronous full-page warming on sub-page reads. Submissions to this pool are bursty (one per S3 GET in the worst case); size accordingly.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.async.processing.enabled
description: If enabled, S3 requests are dispatched off the Netty event loop into the async light/heavy thread pools. Keeping work off the event loop prevents a single slow request from wedging all keep-alive connections pinned to that loop. Disable only for diagnostic purposes.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.audit.logging.enabled
description: Set to true to enable worker netty s3 audit.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.audit.logging.queue.capacity
description: Capacity of the queue used by the worker S3 audit logging writer.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.authentication.enabled
description: Whether to enable check s3 rest request header.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.authentication.session.provider
description: The session provider type for S3 authentication.OIDC: the session token is generated by OIDC provider.ALLUXIO_STS: use temporary session token generated by Alluxio Security Token Service.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.authenticator.classname
description: The class's name is instantiated as an S3 authenticator.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.authorization.enabled

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.authorizer.classname

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucket.naming.restrictions.enabled
description: Toggles whether or not the Alluxio S3 API will enforce AWS S3 bucket naming restrictions. See https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/bucketnamingrules.html.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.bucketpathcache.timeout
description: Per-worker TTL for the positive cache that records whether an Alluxio S3 bucket path exists and is a directory. Disabled by default ("0min") to match AWS S3 read-after-write consistency for bucket lifecycle. The cache is consulted by the bucket-existence checks that remain on the bucket-level metadata path (HeadBucket / GetBucketLocation / GetBucketVersioning / ListMultipartUploads / DeleteBucket and the few remaining checkPathIsAlluxioDirectory call sites in object-level operations); enabling it (e.g. "60s") trades a bounded staleness window for fewer bucket-validate RPCs on those paths. Only positive (exists) entries are cached — missing buckets are not negatively cached so a freshly-created bucket isn't masked until TTL expiry. The 404-disambiguation helpers in NettyRestUtils do not consult this cache; they always re-probe.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.complete.multipart.upload.min.part.size
description: The minimum required file size of parts for multipart uploads. Parts which are smaller than this limit aside from the final part will result in an EntityTooSmall error code. Set to 0 to disable size requirements.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.idle.max.time
description: Specify the max keep-alive time for the HTTP connection in S3 API. If the channel has been idle for the specified time, the connection will be closed. If set it as 0, the connection will not actively expire.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.connection.keep.alive.enabled
description: If enabled the connection keep-alive, S3 API will maintain the TCP connection to support the client to reuse it and send new requests.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.deletetype
description: Delete type when deleting buckets and objects through S3 API. Valid options are `%s` (delete both in Alluxio and UFS), `%s` (delete only the buckets or objects in Alluxio namespace).

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.header.metadata.max.size
description: The maximum size to allow for user-defined metadata in S3 PUTrequest headers. Set to 0 to disable size limits.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.https.port
description: The HTTPs port for Alluxio worker's s3 api.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.list.directory.as.object.enabled
description: Toggles whether the Alluxio S3 API will include directories as 0-byte objects in ListObjects/ListObjectsV2 responses. When true (the default), every directory encountered during listing is returned as a 0-byte entry in Contents (Alluxio's historical behavior). When false, the prefix path itself is suppressed and synthesized parent folders (folders with no backing UFS object, identified by a zero lastModificationTimeMs) are dropped, while real 0-byte directory marker objects (folders carrying a positive mtime plumbed through from the underlying object's LastModified) are preserved. Listings with a delimiter are unaffected: directories continue to surface via CommonPrefixes.

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.only.https.access
description: Set to true to only provide the HTTPs port and disable the default HTTP port for s3 api

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.only.read.cache.data.enabled

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.receive.packet.size

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.signature.validation.enabled

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.tls.enabled

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.transfer.packet.size

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.write.redirect.enabled

group_title: Worker S3 API 网关
name: alluxio.worker.s3.writetype

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.region.backpressure.enabled
description: If true, count FileRegion bytes toward the netty data-server's outbound buffer watermark, so WriteBufferWaterMark backpressure bounds per-channel FileRegion queue depth. Bounds the worker's open-FD count per in-flight S3 GET regardless of object size. Disable only as an emergency rollback if the size-estimator change interacts badly with a specific workload; revert restores the previous behaviour where FileRegions are treated as zero-sized and the outbound queue can grow with response size.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.file.transfer
description: When returning files to the user, select how the data is transferred; valid options are `MAPPED` (uses java MappedByteBuffer) and `TRANSFER` (uses Java FileChannel.transferTo).

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.queue.enabled
description: Whether netty buffer queue is enabled

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.handler.thread.idle.timeout
description: The maximum time a worker keeps a handler thread ready for listening to client requests on an open channel. If no requests occur within this time, the idle thread is released. Shorter values improve thread utilization but may incur a scheduling overhead when a new request comes in after the thread has been released.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.enabled
description: Whether the worker periodically logs in-flight Netty read requests that have been stuck in a non-terminal state longer than . Logging only; helps diagnose reads that never terminate (and so never emit a close log).

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.read.watchdog.stuck.threshold
description: How long an in-flight Netty read request may make no progress (no state transition) before the watchdog reports it as wedged. Defaults to roughly twice (default 10s) — a read stalled longer than the client's per-response timeout, twice over. The scan interval is derived from this value, so there is no separate interval to configure.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.buffer.size.packets
description: The maximum number of parallel data packets when a client reads from a worker.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.queue.size

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.reader.thread.keep.alive.time

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

description: The quiet period. When the netty server is shutting down, it will ensure that no RPCs occur during the quiet period. If an RPC occurs, then the quiet period will restart before shutting down the netty server.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

description: Backing queue for the netty UFS-writer thread pool. HANDOFF_QUEUE pairs with BoundedHandoffExecutor; other values use a plain ThreadPoolExecutor. Queue size (when bounded) comes from .

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.worker.threads

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.buffer.size.packets

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.size
description: The size of the queue in the writer thread pool. Shared by both the writer and UFS-writer pools.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.queue.type

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.thread.keep.alive.time

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.core

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.netty.writer.threads.max

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.permit.keepalive.time
description: Specify the most aggressive keep-alive time clients are permitted to configure. The server will try to detect clients exceeding this rate and when detected will forcefully close the connection.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.reader.buffer.pooled

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

If this value is lower than read chunk size, read performance may be impacted as worker waits more often for buffer to free up. Higher value will increase the memory consumed by each read request.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.network.writer.buffer.size.messages

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.fjp.async

This mode may be more appropriate than default locally stack-based mode in applications in which worker threads only process event-style asynchronous tasks.

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.keepalive

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.max.pool.size

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.tpe.allow.core.threads.timeout

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web

group_title: Worker 网络 / RPC / Web
name: alluxio.worker.rpc.executor.type
description: Type of ExecutorService for Alluxio worker gRPC server. Supported values are TPE (for ThreadPoolExecutor) and FJP (for ForkJoinPool).

Increase this to buffer more tasks at the cost of higher latency signaling.

name: alluxio.worker.identity.uuid.file.path
description: The path to the file containing the identity of the worker specified as a UUID. This path should be readable and writable to the worker process. Worker instances in a Alluxio cluster must have different identities.

name: alluxio.worker.job.load.executor.threads.max

description: Whether to enable start JVM monitor thread on the worker. This will start a thread to detect JVM-wide pauses induced by GC or other reasons.

group_title: Worker 生命周期 / 成员 / 注册 / Rebalance
name: alluxio.worker.load.file.partition.size
description: If set to a value greater than 0, during the distributed load, worker will partition the file into small slices and load them concurrently. Please consider turn on this feature if the file size is large but the file amount is small.

name: alluxio.worker.loading.from.ufs.dedup.enabled
description: If enabled, will do dedup when multiple cold read requests try reading the same page from UFS. There'll be only one thread loading the data and others will block until the load finishes.

name: alluxio.worker.mark.writing.files.duration
description: The duration a worker keeps the 'writing' mark for a file. The timer resets on every write. If no write occurs within this duration, the mark is removed.

group_title: Worker 生命周期 / 成员 / 注册 / Rebalance
name: alluxio.worker.master.connect.retry.timeout

group_title: Worker 生命周期 / 成员 / 注册 / Rebalance
name: alluxio.worker.master.periodical.rpc.timeout
description: Timeout for periodical RPC between workers and the leading master. This property is added to prevent workers from hanging in periodical RPCs with previous leading master during flaky network situations. If the timeout is too short, periodical RPCs may not have enough time to get response from the leading master during heavy cluster load and high network latency.

group_title: Worker 生命周期 / 成员 / 注册 / Rebalance

name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.queue
description: Queue capacity for the range-miss blob backfill pool (see ).

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.range.prefetch.threads
description: Max concurrent background full-blob backfills triggered by range misses. Saturation skips the backfill (the range was already served from upstream); raise for K8s cold-start bursts where many nodes range-miss the same large image at once.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.singleflight.timeout
description: Maximum time a blob singleflight follower waits for the leader's cold pull to populate the cache before falling through to its own direct upstream fetch. A follower fall-through costs one extra upstream GET; the old 10min value could stall a stampede of clients on a wedged leader. Raise it only for genuinely huge (multi-GB) layers over a slow upstream.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.blob.stream.timeout
description: Upper bound a blob streamer thread waits for a single chunk write to the client channel to complete under backpressure. Caps how long a slow or vanished client can pin a streamer thread; pairs with the HTTP idle timeout.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.negative.ttl
description: TTL of negative (deny) AuthZ cache entries. Implemented via a scheduled invalidate so a deny verdict yields the cache sooner than a normal positive entry; lets a user that just received new permissions pull successfully without waiting for the full positive TTL.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.authz.ttl
description: TTL of the repo-level AuthZ short cache (positive verdicts). Shorter values make permission revocations propagate faster at the cost of more upstream auth checks. Tokens that expire sooner than this value will still be honoured by the cache up to the configured TTL, so do not raise it above the typical upstream token lifetime.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.binding.ttl
description: TTL of the repo-scoped object binding cache (digest ↔ repo safety fence) AND of the blob metadata cache. The two are intentionally linked: a metadata entry must never outlive its binding, otherwise the handler could serve cached bytes whose authorization fence has expired. Longer values reduce upstream HEAD traffic; shorter values force more frequent re-validation.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.blacklist.etcd.path
description: Etcd parent path where the OCI mirror watches for cache-bypass entries. Each child key represents one URL-escaped `<registry>/<repo>` (exact-match) that should bypass both the in-memory manifest/blob meta caches and the worker page store. Used to opt out frequently-mutated images (e.g. tags like `latest`, `developer`, or large Windows base images) from cache logic so that tag rewrites take effect immediately. Workers update their view via an etcd watch.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.hard.ttl
description: Hard TTL for tag → digest bindings. After this age the entry is fully evicted and the next request takes the slow GET-manifest path.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.cache.tag.soft.ttl
description: Soft TTL for tag → digest bindings. Below this age, cached bindings are served without contacting upstream. After this age but before the hard TTL, the binding is still served but the next request triggers an upstream HEAD revalidation so a tag that has been re-pushed is detected promptly. Must be ≤ the hard TTL.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.enabled
description: Whether to enable the OCI Distribution V2 registry mirror (/v2/...) on the worker HTTP server. When enabled, the worker fronts an upstream OCI registry such as Azure ACR. See design/Azure-ACR/alluxio_acr_v02_refined_full_design_cn.md.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.persistent.cache.enabled
description: Whether the OCI registry handler decides cache hits by probing the worker page store directly (via CacheManager.getUsage) instead of consulting the in-memory mBlobMetaCache. When true, blob bytes that survive a worker restart can still be served from the on-disk cache, at the cost of one upstream HEAD request per cold (in-memory) lookup. Recommended for production once validated.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.allowlist
description: Comma-separated allowlist of upstream registry hostnames the OCI mirror is permitted to forward to (e.g. "myacr.azurecr.io,secondacr.azurecr.io"). Empty means "allow any host" -- useful for development but unsafe in production, since a misconfigured oci-proxy could otherwise direct the worker to fetch from arbitrary internet hosts.

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.oci.registry.upstream.throttle.cooldown
description: When the upstream registry returns a 429/503 rate-limit response, the worker records a short per-(registry, repo) cooldown and serves an immediate 429 (with Retry-After) to further requests for that repo instead of hammering the already-throttling upstream. The cooldown honors the upstream Retry-After header but is capped at this value. 0 disables the local cooldown (Retry-After is still propagated to clients).

group_title: Worker 数据格式加速 (OCI/Parquet/Preload)
name: alluxio.worker.parquet.max.object.size
description: Sets the maximum object graph size (number of objects) Ehcache will traverse during sizeof calculation for Parquet Raw cache entries. This helps prevent traversing very large object graphs when estimating cache size.

name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.remote
description: Boolean indicating whether or not the under storage worker nodes are remote with respect to Alluxio worker nodes. If set to true, Alluxio will not attempt to discover locality information from the under storage because locality is impossible. This will improve performance. The default value is true.

name: alluxio.underfs.hdfs.trash.enabled

Note that HDFS must be configured to enable the trash functionality (in 'core-site.xml'). If trashing is not enabled in HDFS, Alluxio will delete the file directly, regardless of this configuration.

group_title: Coordinator / Master 元数据与调度
name: alluxio.coordinator.rebalance.job.history.max.retained
description: The maximum number of finished rebalance jobs retained in etcd. When a new rebalance job is launched, older finished jobs beyond this limit are deleted, oldest first. A value of 0 or less disables pruning (unbounded history). Each retained job record is roughly 5-10 KB for a small membership change and up to about 1-2 MB for a large replace across many workers; the retained history occupies approximately this count multiplied by the per-job size in etcd.

group_title: Coordinator / Master 元数据与调度
name: alluxio.coordinator.rebalance.job.worker.timeout
description: The worker timeout of the rebalance job.

group_title: Coordinator / Master 元数据与调度
name: alluxio.coordinator.scheduler.fast.refill.enabled
description: If true, the coordinator refills a job's per-worker tasks up to x active workers (x the disorder tolerance) instead of a fixed 3, giving higher concurrency and slow-node tolerance on the distributed-load path.

group_title: Coordinator / Master 元数据与调度
name: alluxio.coordinator.scheduler.sync.job.threads.max
description: The number of workers that can register at the same time. Others will wait and retry until they are granted a RegisterLease. If you observe pressure on the master when many workers start up and register, tune down this parameter.

name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.fraction
description: Fraction of a tenant's per-second limit that each FUSE client fetches from the token server per refill (one RPC amortized across the batch). Larger values mean fewer refill RPCs and lower stall risk (favoring throughput) but let a client hold more unused tokens locally (looser cross-client accuracy); smaller values are tighter but chattier. The result is clamped to the configured min/max batch size and never exceeds the per-second limit itself.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.max.size
description: Upper bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server. Caps how many tokens a single client can hold locally, bounding cross-client inaccuracy at the cost of more refill RPCs at very high limits.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.batch.min.size
description: Lower bound on the per-refill token batch a FUSE client fetches from the rate-limit token server.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.enabled
description: Whether the cluster-wide per-user FUSE rate limiter is enabled. When false the limiter is not constructed and adds zero overhead on the I/O path.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.cluster.rate.limit.fallback.to.local
description: When the rate-limit token server is unreachable, whether each FUSE client falls back to enforcing the limit locally (fail-open). When false, requests block until the server is reachable again.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群

Our microbench test shows that 1GB cache can double the metadata read operation performance for a meta store that persists 10M metadata entries. Note that the memory is off-heap and does not count in jvm memory.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.interval
description: The delay between PageMetaCF tombstone-compaction rounds. Each round compacts at most a few of the densest SST files, so a short interval keeps tombstone pressure low while a single round stays cheap.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.min.tombstones
description: An SST file in PageMetaCF is eligible for tombstone compaction only when it holds at least this many delete tombstones. Guards against compacting small files whose high tombstone ratio represents little reclaimable space.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.compaction.tombstone.ratio

name: alluxio.dora.worker.page.meta.store.rocksdb.enabled

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.etcd.endpoints
description: A list of comma-separated http://host:port addresses of etcd cluster (e.g. http://localhost:2379,http://etcd1:2379)

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time
description: gRPC HTTP/2 keepalive ping interval for the jetcd client. When the channel is idle for this long, a PING is sent so a stalled etcd endpoint (e.g. during defrag) is detected and the client can recover the gRPC channel. Must be >= the etcd server's --grpc-keepalive-min-time (default 5s on etcd) or the server will send GOAWAY frames. To preserve an in-flight lease across a stall, this value plus alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout should be less than the lease TTL (alluxio.worker.failure.detection.timeout for workers, default 15s); otherwise this setting only bounds recovery time, it does not save the lease. With defaults (10s+3s=13s detection, 15s lease) the margin is a tight 2s — lower this to 5s for a 7s margin if your network can tolerate the extra keepalive PINGs.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.timeout
description: How long the jetcd client waits for a keepalive PONG before marking the gRPC channel as broken. Lower values detect stalled endpoints faster but increase false-positive disconnects on lossy networks. Total detection latency is alluxio.etcd.keepalive.time + this value.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.etcd.keepalive.without.calls
description: Whether the jetcd client sends keepalive pings even when no RPCs are in flight. Required for failover when the only active stream (e.g. LeaseKeepAlive) is itself stalled on a hung etcd endpoint.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群
name: alluxio.etcd.max.inbound.message.size
description: The maximum inbound message size for Etcd client.

group_title: 成员管理 / etcd / 一致性哈希 / 集群

name: alluxio.network.host.resolution.timeout
description: During startup of the Master and Worker processes Alluxio needs to ensure that they are listening on externally resolvable and reachable host names. To do this, Alluxio will automatically attempt to select an appropriate host name if one was not explicitly specified. This represents the maximum amount of time spent waiting to determine if a candidate host name is resolvable over the network.

group_title: 全局网络 / gRPC / 传输
name: alluxio.network.netty.heartbeat.timeout
description: The amount of time the server will wait before closing a netty connection if there has not been any incoming traffic. The client will periodically heartbeat when there is no activity on a connection. This value should be the same on the clients and server.

group_title: 全局网络 / gRPC / 传输
name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.device
description: The RDMA device name (e.g. 'mlx5_0:1') that Alluxio's RDMA transport should use for both the UCX listener and data transfer. Device names can be obtained via 'ibv_devinfo' (format: device_name:port_number). When a single device is specified, the corresponding network interface IP is auto-resolved for the UCX listener bind address and registered as the RDMA connect host. Multiple devices can be specified as a comma-separated list (e.g. 'mlx5_0:1,mlx5_1:1'); in that case the listener falls back to alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.host. If unset, UCX will auto-select all available devices.

group_title: 全局网络 / gRPC / 传输
name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.bind.host

name: alluxio.network.rdma.data.buffer.pool.prewarm.size

description: Controls the errno FUSE returns when a rename or hard link is rejected because the source and destination are in different write-cache directory-quota domains. When false (default), it returns EOPNOTSUPP and the operation fails outright without copying. When true, it returns EXDEV, so coreutils mv transparently falls back to copy+delete (the copy is re-created and counted in the destination domain).

group_title: FUSE 挂载
name: alluxio.user.fuse.hard.link.return.exdev.enabled
description: Enables a workaround for RocksDB versions older than 7.1.1 that do not handle EOPNOTSUPP correctly. When enabled, Alluxio FUSE will return EXDEV instead of EOPNOTSUPP for hard link operations. This is primarily needed for compatibility with older RocksDB versions (e.g., dependencies in integration tests). Newer RocksDB versions do not require this setting.

group_title: FUSE 挂载

description: If enabled, FUSE getxattr requests for well-known probe xattrs such as security.capability and system.posix_acl_* are answered locally with ENODATA instead of querying the backing filesystem.

group_title: FUSE 挂载
name: alluxio.user.fuse.multipart.upload.enabled
description: If enabled, when writing a file to UFS by fuse, data will be partitioned into multiple parts and uploaded in parallel. This improves the write performance for large files if the network bandwidth is sufficient. This property tries to do multipart upload on a best effort. If the UFS doesn't support so, the upload will fall back to regular mode.

This mode may be more appropriate than default locally stack-based mode in applications in which master secure threads only process event-style asynchronous tasks.

group_title: 安全 / 认证 / 授权 / 审计

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.concurrent.write.enabled
description: Whether to enable concurrent writing. When this configuration is set to true, it will simultaneously change the behavior of both FUSE and the worker: FUSE will convert all write locks into read locks, and the worker’s handle will allow writing to multiple files concurrently.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.coordinator.heartbeat.threads
description: The number of threads in the Coordinator to poll and manage worker quota usage.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.enabled
description: Whether quota feature is enabled

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.etcd.polling.interval.ms
description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest quota definitions. By default the interval is 3s.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.evictor.capacity.modifier.size
description: This configuration tweaks the behavior of the evictor which restores quota usage. A positive value like 64MB means the evictor will try to free 64MiB ahead of time on each worker. A negative value like -1MB means the evictor will tolerate each worker to use 1MB more than the capacity. The default value is 1MB, which means after eviction, the quota usage will still be slightly more than the quota definition, so the quota stays full.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.limit.exceeded.action
description: The action to take when usage under a quota limit is exceeded.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.worker.heartbeat.interval.ms
description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the interval between two polls.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.quota.worker.heartbeat.timeout.ms
description: The master polls all workers for the latest quota usage regularly. This property specifies the worker needs to respond the quota usage within this timeout.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.ttl.eviction.check.interval

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.ttl.policy.enabled
description: Whether TTL policy feature is enabled

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.ttl.policy.etcd.polling.interval.ms
description: Interval for polling ETCD for the latest TTL policy definitions. By default the interval is 3s.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.async.persist.enabled
description: Whether to enable asynchronous persistence of files from the write buffer to the underlying storage. If false, files are persisted upon close.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.buffer.number
description: The number of buffers in the pool for write buffer multipart upload. Set to 0 to disable pooling: a new buffer is allocated on demand and dropped on release instead of being reused.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.enabled
description: Whether to enable multipart upload for the write buffer file system. When enabled, large writes are buffered in memory and flushed as part files, then merged at the metadata level on close.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.max.buffers.per.stream
description: The maximum number of buffers a single multipart upload stream can hold concurrently. If the stream has reached this limit, further buffer allocation will block until an in-flight part write completes and returns its buffer. Set to 0 for unlimited (no per-stream limit).

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.part.size
description: The size of each part in bytes for write buffer multipart upload.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.buffer.multipart.upload.threads
description: The number of threads used for uploading parts in write buffer multipart upload.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.check.orphan.timeout
description: This configuration specifies the timeout duration for orphan file detection.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.file.check.period
description: The period for checking files in the write buffer. Files that have been in the buffer longer than this period will be prioritized for persistence.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.cache.free.delay
description: The delay time for releasing the cache after data persistence is completed. A value less than or equal to 0 disables delayed release.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.schedule.period
description: The scheduling period for the asynchronous persistence service to check for and persist files from the write buffer.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.submit.batch.size
description: The batch size of submitting async persist tasks.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.async.persist.thread.pool.size
description: The size of the thread pool for asynchronously persisting files from the write buffer to the underlying storage.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.compaction.enabled
description: Whether to enable write log compaction for the write cache. When enabled, write logs within a block will be periodically merged to reduce the number of log entries and reclaim storage space.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.dual.buffer.file.system.type
description: The type of the dual write buffer file system.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.enabled
description: Whether to enable the write buffer feature. When enabled, writes will go through a temporary buffer on workers before being persisted to the underlying storage.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.file.metadata.storage
description: The storage type for write buffer file metadata. Options are `MEMORY` for in-memory storage (for testing) and `FDB` for FoundationDB.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.local.file.system.path
description: The local file system path for write buffer.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.enabled
description: Whether to wrap the singleton FDB metastore instance with the local read-through cache.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.negative.ttl
description: The TTL for negative entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache. Set to 0sec to disable negative-entry caching.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.metastore.cache.positive.ttl
description: The TTL for positive entries in the write-cache metastore read-through cache.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.block.size
description: The block size used by the write cache. Write logs and page store files are organized and compacted at block-level granularity.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.cluster.name
description: The name of the PFS cluster. Used to namespace FDB subspaces so that multiple clusters can share the same FoundationDB instance.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.batch.delay
description: Test/debug throttle: an artificial delay a directory-quota backfill worker sleeps after each processed batch, slowing the backfill (regardless of subtree shape) so the COMPUTING/CLEARING window stays open long enough to observe or exercise concurrent operations. Default 0 disables it; leave it 0 in production.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.batch
description: How many child inodes a directory-quota backfill worker stamps (or strips) per FoundationDB transaction. The ceiling is driven by transaction-conflict waste, not FDB's 5s/10MB limits: a larger batch redoes more work when it collides with online writes, nested backfills, or async deletion on the same inodes. Lower it for deeply nested quotas or when backfill overlaps large recursive deletes.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.scan.interval
description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader polls the backfill/unbackfill queues for items to dispatch and for COMPUTING/CLEARING quotas whose queues have drained.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.backfill.worker.threads
description: Size of the directory-quota backfill worker thread pool. Bounds the number of concurrent backfill write transactions against FoundationDB.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.cache.refresh.interval
description: How often the in-memory directory-quota cache is reloaded from FoundationDB. The cache is consulted on every write hot path instead of issuing per-write quota reads, so a shorter interval yields tighter enforcement at the cost of more background scans. setDirectoryQuota and clearDirectoryQuota refresh the cache synchronously in the calling process; other processes (e.g. the coordinator when a mutation is issued from a CLI JVM) catch up on their next scheduled tick — i.e. within this interval.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.counter.anchor.bits
description: Log2 of the number of anchor positions each directory-quota usage counter is scattered across in the FoundationDB inode keyspace (2^value anchors; default 8 = 256). More anchors spread a hot quota's counter writes over more storage teams, but cost proportionally more reads on cache refresh and getDirectoryQuota, so match it to the cluster's storage-team count. Expressed as a power of two so it can only change by integer multiples across versions, keeping the anchor set nested (a rolling restart stays correct once complete); values below 8 are raised to 8.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.directory.quota.orphan.cleanup.interval
description: How often the directory-quota backfill leader sweeps leaked usage counters: counter slots whose quota id no longer has a limit row (left behind when a non-empty quota-bearing directory is deleted and its descendants' delete-time decrements recreate slots after the inline cleanup removed the row). The leak is benign for enforcement (a quota with no limit row is never loaded into the enforce cache), so this is a low-frequency, best-effort sweep that runs only when the leader's queues are idle.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.id.allocator.batch.size
description: The number of IDs to allocate per FDB transaction in the inode and write log ID allocators. A larger batch reduces FDB transactions but increases the gap in ID sequences when a node restarts.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.scatter.buckets
description: The number of scatter buckets used by the inode ID allocator. This value is persisted in the cluster info table on first initialization and subsequent restarts will use the persisted value.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.pfs.worker.id.cache.size
description: The maximum number of entries cached in each direction of the worker-id to long-reference mapping (see WorkerIdMapper). Bounds memory while avoiding FDB point lookups for recently seen worker references.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.refresh.enabled
description: Whether to allow the on-demand `ttl refresh` command, which walks a path prefix and retroactively stamps the write-cache TTL onto already-cached files. Disabled by default; enable deliberately, as a refresh can walk a large subtree.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.batch.size
description: Number of TTL index entries fetched per FDB range-scan page inside the write-cache TTL scanner.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval
description: How often the write-cache TTL scanner runs. Defaults to 1h to match the read-cache TTL eviction cadence (alluxio.ttl.eviction.check.interval); lower it for more prompt write-cache reclaim at higher coordinator/FDB scan cost, but not below unless that floor is also lowered.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.interval.min
description: Floor on the effective write-cache TTL scan interval; defaults to 1h to match the scan-interval default, so by default the scanner runs no more often than hourly. A configured below this floor is clamped up to it (with a warning), which also prevents an accidental tiny interval from tight-spinning the scanner. To scan more frequently, lower this floor too — do so cautiously: scanning faster than the drain can reclaim only adds coordinator/FDB load with no benefit.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.max.per.round
description: Maximum number of expired files the write-cache TTL scanner processes per scheduled run.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scan.threads
description: Number of threads the write-cache TTL scanner uses to purge expired files concurrently within a round. Higher values drain a backlog faster; each thread runs independent per-inode FDB transactions, made safe by the unlink CAS. Set to 1 for the legacy serial scan.

group_title: 写入 / TTL / 配额
name: alluxio.write.cache.ttl.scanner.enabled
description: Whether to enable the background write-cache TTL scanner that automatically purges expired files from the TRANSIENT write cache.

group_title: JVM / 进程 / 系统级 / 杂项
name: <unresolved:TEST_DEPRECATED_KEY>

group_title: JVM / 进程 / 系统级 / 杂项
name: alluxio.leak.detector.level
description: Set this to one of {DISABLED, SIMPLE, ADVANCED, PARANOID} to track resource leaks in the Alluxio codebase. DISABLED does not track any leaks. SIMPLE only samples resources, and doesn't track recent accesses, having a low overhead. ADVANCED is like simple, but tracks recent object accesses and has higher overhead. PARANOID tracks all objects and has the highest overhead. It is recommended to only use this value during testing.
